texto: So the Bawdy Bluestocking was the proprietress of her own shop, selling lurid novels to ladies in the front and more esoteric fare in the back, from the looks of the shelves around him. He spied Pope and Crabbe, Shakespeare, of course, and names he did not recognize at all. He wondered how she chose her stock and where it came from. She must spend her days in endless research. The thought was unaccountably lovely to him.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Evelyn Pryce sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “So the Bawdy Bluestocking was the proprietress of her own shop, selling lurid novels to ladies in…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, historical-romance, regency-romance, útil em momentos de termino e regency-romance.
